_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
This regulatory cluster positions the Georgia EPD as the primary state environmental regulator imposing compliance obligations on any entity that owns, occupies, or directs operations at physical sites in Georgia — including warehouses, logistics yards, staging grounds, ramp areas, and temporary production facilities.

As a freight forwarder, the workspace occupies the role of **operator and/or responsible party** for any Georgia-based facility, vehicle staging area, or client-site operation it controls. Under the Summer Open Burning Ban (O.C.G.A. Chapter 391-3-1, EPD Air Rules), the workspace is classified as a "citizen or business" subject to the prohibition on burning yard and land-clearing debris during the ozone season. This obligation runs with physical site control — if the workspace manages or directs operations at a Georgia location in a covered county, compliance responsibility attaches regardless of whether burning is performed by a subcontractor or client.

Under the Drought Response Level 1 declaration (Georgia DNR Drought Management Rules, Chapter 391-3-30), the workspace is indirectly affected as a downstream user of public water systems at any Georgia facility. Public water systems serving those facilities are the direct obligors for public information campaigns; the workspace itself faces no direct filing obligation at Level 1. However, if escalation to Level 2 occurs, outdoor water use restrictions would directly apply to workspace-operated facilities on public supply.

For NPDES Construction Stormwater permitting (General Permits GAR100001/GAR100002/GAR100003), the workspace holds the role of **Permittee/Operator** for any construction activity disturbing one or more acres at a Georgia location. The workspace must file a Notice of Intent through GEOS and maintain a Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SWPPP) prior to ground disturbance.

*Role confirmation requiring legal review:* Where the workspace acts as a logistics services provider at a client-controlled site (e.g., a film production lot or event venue), the delineation of operator responsibility for open burning and stormwater compliance between the workspace and the site owner/client should be confirmed in contract language and legal counsel review.

*Source: Georgia EPD Press Release — Annual Ban on Open Burning, Georgia EPD, April 28, 2025. https://epd.georgia.gov/press-releases/2025-04-28/annual-open-burning; Georgia EPD Press Release — Level 1 Drought Declared, Georgia EPD, April 27, 2026. https://epd.georgia.gov/press-releases/2026-04-27/level-1-drought-declared; NPDES Construction Stormwater General Permits, Georgia EPD. https://epd.georgia.gov/forms-permits/watershed-protection-branch-forms-permits/storm-water-forms/npdes-construction.*
